verses.commentary: Paul could not address them as spiritual but as fleshly, as infants in Christ. Chronological time in Christ does not equal spiritual maturity.
Milk suits infants, solid food requires maturity. Their inability to handle solid teaching revealed ongoing immaturity, not growth.
Jealousy and quarreling prove they remain worldly, acting like mere humans. Division evidences carnality, not spirituality.
Identifying with human leaders (I follow Paul, I follow Apollos) demonstrates they are acting as mere humans, trapped in fleshly thinking.
Paul and Apollos are merely servants through whom they believed, each assigned their task by the Lord. Ministers are instruments, not objects of devotion.
Paul planted, Apollos watered, but God made it grow. Human workers contribute, but God alone gives life and growth.
Neither planter nor waterer is anything; only God who gives growth matters. Human ministry has value only as God empowers it.
Planter and waterer are one in purpose, though each receives reward according to their own labor. Unity of purpose with diversity of reward.
We are God's fellow workers; you are God's field, God's building. Believers are both the field where God works and the building he constructs.
By God's grace, Paul laid foundation as wise builder; others build on it. Each must be careful how they build on the foundation.
No one can lay another foundation than what is laid: Jesus Christ. All ministry must build on Christ alone, no alternative exists.
Building materials range from precious (gold, silver, costly stones) to worthless (wood, hay, straw). Quality of work varies drastically.
Each work will be revealed by the Day when fire tests its quality. Time and testing reveal true substance versus appearance.
If the work survives fire, the builder receives reward. Lasting work receives lasting reward.
If work burns, the builder suffers loss but is saved, though only as through fire. Bad work results in loss of reward but not loss of salvation.
Don't you know you are God's temple and God's Spirit dwells in you? The community, not individuals, forms God's temple.
If anyone destroys God's temple, God will destroy that person. The temple is sacred, and you together are that temple. Damaging the church incurs divine judgment.
Don't deceive yourselves. If anyone thinks they are wise in this age, they should become fools to become truly wise. Worldly wisdom must be rejected to gain true wisdom.
This world's wisdom is foolishness with God. Scripture says he catches the wise in their craftiness, showing divine perspective on human wisdom.
Again, the Lord knows the thoughts of the wise are futile. God sees through intellectual pretension to its essential emptiness.
Therefore stop boasting about human leaders. All things are yours, removing any need to claim allegiance to specific teachers.
Whether Paul, Apollos, Cephas, the world, life, death, present, or future, all belongs to you. Believers inherit everything, transcending partisan divisions.
You belong to Christ, and Christ belongs to God. The chain of belonging places all humanity and ministry in proper hierarchy.
